Good or glowing skin quality.

Number of cervical-fluid self-observations made on this day. Creighton FertilityCare protocol records the most-fertile observation of the day plus the count of times observations were made (typically each bathroom visit).
description.fr: Nombre d'auto-observations de la glaire effectuées dans la journée. Le protocole Creighton FertilityCare enregistre l'observation la plus fertile de la journée et le nombre de contrôles effectués (typiquement à chaque passage aux toilettes).

description.en: Manual strech test of cervical fluid. Short can be streched less than 2 cm, long greater than 2cm.
Vulva wetness feeling
Vulva wetness when wiping
Subjective bleeding intensity on a 0-1 proportion scale.
Presence of brown or dark coloration in bleeding. Qualifier for a co-occurring body-vulva-bleeding event.
Presence of blood clots during bleeding. Qualifier for a co-occurring bleeding event.
Vaginal bleeding following childbirth (lochia).
Vaginal bleeding associated with pregnancy loss.
Cup fill level when emptied.
description.fr: Niveau de remplissage de la coupe au moment de vider.

description.en: Self-checked cervix — height, firmness, openness, and tilt.
description.fr: Col auto-examiné — hauteur, fermeté, ouverture et inclinaison.

description.en: Measured body weight
description.fr: Poids corporel mesuré

description.en: Resting body temperature taken on waking, before any activity. Used for fertility charting.
description.fr: Température au repos prise au réveil, avant toute activité. Utilisée pour le suivi de la fertilité.

description.en: Measured body height
description.fr: Taille mesuré

description.en: Sperm concentration in semen (number of spermatozoa per mL of ejaculate). Stored in megacounts per millilitre (10⁶/mL).
description.fr: Concentration de spermatozoïdes dans le sperme (nombre par mL d'éjaculat). Stockée en mégacomptes par millilitre (10⁶/mL).

description.en: Fraction of progressively-motile sperm (PR) in semen — sperm moving actively in a straight line or large circle, regardless of speed. Distinct from total motility (PR+NP). Used as the multiplier in Total Motile Count derivation (concentration × volume × this).
description.fr: Fraction de spermatozoïdes à mobilité progressive (PR) dans le sperme — spermatozoïdes se déplaçant activement en ligne droite ou en large cercle, quelle que soit la vitesse. Distinct de la mobilité totale (PR+NP). Utilisé comme multiplicateur dans le calcul du nombre total de spermatozoïdes mobiles (concentration × volume × cette valeur).

description.en: Fraction of motile sperm (progressive PR + non-progressive NP) in semen. Used clinically to diagnose asthenozoospermia. Distinct from progressive motility (PR only). Both can co-exist on the same lab report.
description.fr: Fraction de spermatozoïdes mobiles (progressifs PR + non-progressifs NP) dans le sperme. Utilisée cliniquement pour le diagnostic d'asthénozoospermie. Distincte de la mobilité progressive (PR uniquement). Les deux peuvent coexister sur le même rapport de laboratoire.

description.en: Fraction of live sperm in semen, measured by eosin-nigrosin staining or hypo-osmotic swelling test. Distinct from motility — a sperm can be alive but immotile. WHO 2021 lists this as a mandatory test when total motility is below the reference limit; it is the key discriminator between asthenozoospermia (low motility, mostly live) and necrozoospermia (low motility, mostly dead).
description.fr: Fraction de spermatozoïdes vivants dans le sperme, mesurée par coloration éosine-nigrosine ou test de gonflement hypo-osmotique. Distincte de la mobilité — un spermatozoïde peut être vivant mais immobile. WHO 2021 la classe comme test obligatoire lorsque la mobilité totale est inférieure à la limite de référence ; clé pour distinguer asthénozoospermie (faible mobilité, majoritairement vivants) et nécrozoospermie (faible mobilité, majoritairement morts).

description.en: Volume of semen in a single ejaculate, in millilitres.
description.fr: Volume de sperme dans un éjaculat unique, en millilitres.

description.en: Total motile sperm count per ejaculate (concentration × volume × progressive-motility fraction). Stored in megacounts (10⁶ sperm).
description.fr: Nombre total de spermatozoïdes mobiles par éjaculat (concentration × volume × fraction mobile progressive). Stocké en mégacomptes (10⁶ spermatozoïdes).

description.en: Fraction of spermatozoa with normal morphology (Kruger strict criteria). Stored as a 0..1 ratio.
description.fr: Fraction de spermatozoïdes ayant une morphologie normale (critères stricts de Kruger). Stockée comme un ratio 0..1.

description.en: DNA Fragmentation Index (DFI) — fraction of spermatozoa with fragmented DNA. Stored as a 0..1 ratio.
description.fr: Indice de fragmentation de l'ADN (DFI) — fraction de spermatozoïdes avec ADN fragmenté. Stockée comme un ratio 0..1.

description.en: Number of children at this date
Number of total charted cycles at this date
Your average menstrual cycle length, in days.
description.fr: Votre durée moyenne de cycle menstruel, en jours.

description.en: Your average period length, in days.
description.fr: Votre durée moyenne des règles, en jours.

description.en: Flag this day as an ovulation day
Flag this day as a new cycle start. First day of full menstrual bleeding (Period)

fertility-cycles-start.devNotes: Cycle duration is determined by next fertility cycle date unless duration property is given.

description.en: Last day of periods. First day of period is given by a new cycle indication.

fertility-cycles-period-end.devNotes: To track start / end of period, use fertility-cycles-start & fertility-cycles-period-end.

description.en: When a women is considered a fertile

fertility-cycles-fertile-window.devNotes: Use duration property to indicate end of fertile window.

description.en: When fertility is at its highest point

DEPRECATED — record each miscarriage as its own `fertility-pregnancy`
event with `content = miscarriage`, `time = LMP for that pregnancy`, and
`duration = LMP→outcome`. Aggregate counts are derived analyst-side from
the count of such events. The legacy aggregate-count item (this one) is
retained for backwards compatibility with pre-existing clients only.
Current fertility intention

label.en: TTA - Not taking risks.  Would take all available measures to end a pregnancy.
TTA - Not taking risks.  Would strongly consider placing baby for adoption.
TTA - Not taking risks.  Would need some time, maybe counseling. Ultimately keeping the pregnancy.
TTA - Not taking risks.  Currently content with family size but a surprise pregnancy would be welcome.
TTW/TTA - "Loosely TTA" known risks are taken in the fertile window. "OOPS" pregnancy would be welcome.
TTW - Charting only for health/curiosity. Unprotected intercourse happens whenever. Pregnancy very welcome.
TTW/TTC - Pregnancy very welcome (moving up the scale in very near future).
TTC - "Excited to start/grow a family TTC" Intentional intercourse every cycle. Excited to start/grow a family. But would not use any fertility treatments if needed.
TTC - "Highly hopeful TTC" Intentional intercourse every cycle. Would consider some but not all fertility treatments if needed.
TTC - " Seriously TTC" Intentional intercourse every cycle. Would pursue any/ALL fertility treatments or procedures if needed.

description.en: Potentially reproductive sexual activity

Genital contact that did not involve intercourse (e.g. external contact,
manual stimulation involving genital exposure). Relevant for fertility-
awareness charting because residual sperm exposure can still result in
pregnancy. Distinct from fertility-sexual-activity, which records
intercourse variants. Both items can co-exist on the same day if both
phenomena occurred.
Pregnancy

fertility-pregnancy.devNotes:
One per fetus (can be multiple events sharing the same `time` for multifetal pregnancies).
The value of `event.time` is the patient's LMP (last menstrual period) date for this
pregnancy — standard OB/GYN dating convention. Derivations:
  - estimated conception ≈ time + 14 days
  - estimated delivery   ≈ time + 280 days (40 weeks)
  - outcome date          = time + duration
When content value is 'ongoing', `event.duration` must be `null`.
For any other value, `event.duration` is the elapsed time from LMP to outcome.
Use 'very-early-loss' for losses with `duration < 6 weeks` (biochemical / pre-clinical
pregnancies, typically detected only by hCG without ultrasound confirmation —
clinically distinct from miscarriage per ESHRE 2022 RPL guideline).
Use 'miscarriage' for clinically-confirmed losses ≥ 6 weeks.

description.en: Per-baby delivery / birth event. One event per baby in the woman's account. Siblings of multifetal pregnancies share `event.time` (= delivery date) and typically share `mode`; per-baby attributes (birth weight, sex, APGAR, NICU stay, neonatal death) vary. Used together with fertility-pregnancy (`event.time` = LMP). A fertility-delivery event only exists when the corresponding fertility-pregnancy.outcome is 'livebirth' or 'stillbirth'. Field vocabularies aligned to LOINC: 73762-7 (mode), 8339-4 (birth weight), 76689-9 (sex assigned at birth), 9274-2 (5-min APGAR).
description.fr: Événement d'accouchement / de naissance par bébé. Un événement par bébé dans le compte de la femme. Les bébés issus d'une grossesse multifœtale partagent `event.time` (= date d'accouchement) et partagent généralement `mode` ; les attributs par bébé (poids de naissance, sexe, APGAR, séjour en USIN, décès néonatal) varient. Utilisé conjointement avec fertility-pregnancy (`event.time` = LMP). Un événement fertility-delivery n'existe que lorsque le fertility-pregnancy.outcome correspondant est 'livebirth' ou 'stillbirth'. Vocabulaires alignés sur LOINC : 73762-7 (mode), 8339-4 (poids de naissance), 76689-9 (sexe à la naissance), 9274-2 (APGAR à 5 minutes).

label.en: Days from birth to neonatal death (leave empty if alive)
label.fr: Jours entre naissance et décès néonatal (laisser vide si en vie)

description.en: Follicle Stimulating Hormone concentration in urine - IU/L
Human Chorionic Gonadotropin concentration in urine - IU/L
Pregnanediol-3-glucuronide concentration in urine - IU/L
Estrone-3-glucuronide concentration in urine - µg/L
Luteinizing Hormone concentration in urine - mg/L
Ovulation Predictor Kit result. Uses test-result/scale (-1 negative, 0 indeterminate, 1 positive). Intermediate values for semi-quantitative results (e.g. 0.5 weak positive).
Home pregnancy test result. Uses test-result/scale (-1 negative, 0 indeterminate, 1 positive).
Problems with walking.
description.fr: Difficultés pour marcher.

description.en: Problems washing or dressing yourself.
description.fr: Difficultés pour vous laver ou vous habiller.

description.en: Problems doing your usual activities — work, study, housework, family, leisure.
description.fr: Difficultés pour vos activités habituelles — travail, études, tâches ménagères, famille, loisirs.

description.en: Free-text medication intake with optional dose and route. Content has an 'intake' sub-object following the same structure as medication/coded-v1.

description.fr: Votre état de santé global aujourd'hui.

activity/plain.description: Plain activity event with no specific content; the activity is defined by the event's stream, time and duration, and possibly description.

attributes/biological-sex.description: An attribute type for the user’s sex

attributes/blood-type.description: An attribute type for the user’s blood type

attributes/skin-type.description: An attribute type for the user’s skin type

audio/attached.description:
The audio source is the file attached to the event (no explicit content defined).
You can use the event's duration to mirror the recording's duration.

audio/url.description: A reference to an audio file online.

frequency.description: The frequency tested in the hearing test. Unit: hz

leftEarSensitivity.description: The sensitivity of the left ear. Unit: dB

rightEarSensitivity.description: The sensitivity of the right ear. Unit: dB

start.description: The start date for the sample. Unit: date in ISO 8601 format

end.description: The end date for the sample. Unit: date in ISO 8601 format

ip.description: The IP address of the source, if any
The IP address of the source, if any

blood-pressure/mmhg-bpm.description: The blood pressure measurement. Pressures are stored in mmHg and the heart rate in bpm

boolean/bool.description: An attribute type for true or false

call/name.description: The contact's name (or a free-form identifier)

clinical/fhir.description: Type to record the clinical information, including an attachment, corresponding to the data of the fhir resource.

displayName.description: The primary display name of the clinical record.

clinicalType.description: An identifier that indicates the type of record, such as allergy or condition record.

fhir.description: The Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources (FHIR) data for this record.

identifier.description: The value from the FHIR resource’s id field.

contact/facebook.description: A Facebook user as specified in the Graph API: https://developers.facebook.com/docs/reference/api/user/

contact/vcard.description: A business card in vCard 2.0-3.x format. See: rfc2425, rfc2426.

count/generic.description: For general items that demand no particular handling.

credentials/pryv-api-endpoint.description: Necessary information to access a Pryv.io account. Url in the format of http(s)://{token}@{apiEndpoint}

encrypted/aes-text-base64.description: AES encrypted payload, with a text key and a Base64 payload.

keyRef.description: A reference (e.g. id, name in keychain) to the key to use for decryption.

hint.description: Alternative to keyRef. A textual hint about which key to use for decryption.

file/attached.description: The file is attached to the event

file/attached-multiple.description: A set of file attached. Structure can be declared in the filenames.

file/url.description: A reference to a file hosted elsewhere

frequency/hz.description: Hertz (also known as cycles per second)

message/facebook.description: A Facebook post. See [Facebook's API docs](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/reference/api/post/) for reference. Facebook properties `message` and `created_time` map to event `description` and `time` respectively. Facebook attached pictures can be directly mapped to attachments. Other Facebook properties such as `link`, `source`, `privacy` are allowed.

source.description: Either a fully qualified "URL" for an external source or a "filename" for a Flash Movie or Video attached to this event.

properties.description: Relative to `source`: a list of properties for an uploaded video, for example, the length of the video.

picture.description: Either a fully qualified "URL" for an external picture or a "filename" for a picture attached to this event.

status-type.description: One of mobile_status_update, created_note, added_photos, added_video, shared_story, created_group, created_event, wall_post, app_created_story, published_story, tagged_in_photo, approved_friend

message/twitter.description: A Twitter post. Twitter property `created_at` maps to event `time`. Other Twitter properties (see [Twitter's API docs](https://developer.twitter.com/en/docs/twitter-api)) are allowed.

note/webclip.description: An HTML-formatted note associated to its source URL.

picture/base64.description: The picture is caried in base64 (utf-8) encoded in string

picture/attached.description: The picture is the image file attached to the event (no explicit content defined). TODO: list accepted formats.

picture/url.description: A reference to a picture file online.

position/wgs84.description: The latest revision of the World Geodetic System (used by GPS).

latitude.description: Unit: degrees north from the equator.

longitude.description: Unit: degrees east from the zero meridian.

horizontalAccuracy.description: The radius of uncertainty for latitude and longitude. Unit: meters. Negative if latitude and longitude are invalid.

verticalAccuracy.description: The radius of uncertainty for altitude. Unit: meters. Negative if altitude is invalid.

speed.description: For informational purposes only. Unit: meters / second. Negative if invalid.

bearing.description: Unit: degrees clockwise from north. Negative if invalid.

reproductive-health/sexual-activity.description: A type to record sexual activity

reproductive-health/menstrual-flow.description: A type to record the menstrual cycles

reproductive-health/mucus-quality.description: A type to record the quality of the mucus

reproductive-health/ovulation.description: A type to record the result of an ovulation test

symptoms/severity.description: A type to record severity of a symptom such as bloating or abdominal cramps.

symptoms/appetite-changes.description: A type to represent change in appetite.

video/attached.description: The video is the file attached to the event (no explicit content defined). TODO: list accepted formats.

video/url.description: A reference to an video file online.

vulva-mucus-inspect/v0.description: DEPRECATED — use vulva-mucus-inspect/9d-vector with converter engine instead. Legacy simple enum kept for backward compatibility with existing data.

vulva-mucus-inspect/9d-vector.description: Cervical fluid observation as a 9-dimensional vector. Content is { vectors, source? }. Used with the euclidian-distance converter engine.

source.description: Provenance: which method produced this vector. Absent = direct vector input.

key.description: Source method identifier (e.g. mira, chartneo, creighton)

sourceData.description: Raw observation from the source method

ratio/proportion.description: A proportion value from 0 to 1. Represents a fraction of a whole: 0 = none, 1 = maximum. Used for subjective scales (e.g. bleeding intensity) where reference points are defined by item options.

test-result/scale.description: Generic qualitative test result on a scale from -1 to 1. Negative values indicate a negative result, 0 indicates indeterminate, positive values indicate a positive result. The magnitude represents intensity or confidence. Common values: -1 (negative), 0 (indeterminate), 1 (positive). Intermediate values can represent semi-quantitative results (e.g., 0.5 for a weak positive). Applicable to any binary or semi-quantitative test: medical (OPK, pregnancy, COVID), environmental, industrial, etc.

pregnancy/detailed.description: Pregnancy. Use 'ongoing' while pregnancy is in progress; otherwise the value reflects how it terminated. 'very-early-loss' covers biochemical / pre-clinical losses with duration <6 weeks LMP (typically hCG-detected only, clinically distinct from miscarriage per ESHRE 2022 RPL guideline).

medication/basic.description: Simple medication intake identified by free-text name. 'intake' follows the same structure as medication/coded-v1 for dose and route.

doseValue.description: Amount per intake (e.g. 2 for '2 tablets', 5 for '5 mL')

doseUnit.description: HDS eventType reference for the dose unit

medication/coded-v1.description: Coded medication intake. 'drug' is the coded identity from datasets-service, 'intake' is the user-entered regimen. Maps to FHIR MedicationStatement: drug → medicationCodeableConcept, intake → dosage.

drug.description: Coded drug identity, populated from datasets-service selection. Contains multiple codes when the same drug is matched across coding systems.

description.description: Localized full description (e.g. 'Aspirin 325 MG Oral Tablet')

codes.description: Coding identifiers across systems. A single drug may have codes in RxNorm (prescribing), SNOMED-CT (clinical), and ATC (classification).

hdsId.description: Unique HDS identifier for this code entry

doseValue.description: Amount per intake (e.g. 2 for '2 tablets', 5 for '5 mL')

doseUnit.description: HDS eventType reference for the dose unit

medication/prescription-v1.description: Medication prescription. Captures what the doctor prescribed: drug identity, dosage, frequency, and instructions.

drug.description: Coded drug identity, populated from datasets-service selection.

description.description: Localized full description (e.g. 'Aspirin 325 MG Oral Tablet')

doseUnit.description: HDS eventType reference for the dose unit

asNeeded.description: True if taken as needed (PRN) rather than on schedule

note.description: Free text instructions (e.g. 'take with food', 'before bedtime')

name.description: Free-text name of the treatment / regimen (e.g. 'IVF', 'Chemotherapy', 'CBT')

count.description: Aggregate intake count over the patient's lifetime. 0 = never. Use for Flavour A intake capture.

treatment/coded-v1.description: Coded treatment regimen. 'regimen' is the coded identity from datasets-service.

regimen.description: Coded regimen identity, populated from datasets-service selection.

codes.description: Coding identifiers across systems (SNOMED-CT, ICD-10-PCS, CPT, …).

count.description: Aggregate intake count over the patient's lifetime. 0 = never. Use for Flavour A intake capture.

procedure/basic.description: Free-text clinical procedure. 'findings' is an open-vocabulary array describing procedure-bound observations (e.g. endometriosis localisation during a laparoscopy).

count.description: Aggregate intake count (Flavour A: lifetime occurrences). 0 = never.

findings.description: Open-vocabulary array of procedure-bound observations. Form preset declares which finding types are surfaced per procedure label.

value.description: Finding value — string, boolean, or nested object as appropriate.

procedure/coded-v1.description: Coded clinical procedure. 'procedure' is the coded identity from datasets-service.

procedure.description: Coded procedure identity, populated from datasets-service selection.

codes.description: Coding identifiers across systems (SNOMED-CT, ICD-10-PCS, CPT, …).

concentration/count-ml.description: Counts per milliliter (raw count). Stored value is the absolute number of items per mL — e.g. 16000000 for a sperm concentration of 16 × 10⁶/mL.

concentration/megacount-ml.description: Megacounts per milliliter (10⁶ counts/mL). Stored value is in millions — e.g. 16 for 16 × 10⁶/mL. Use the long-form 'mega' prefix to stay within the all-lowercase eventType convention while encoding the SI Mega (10⁶) multiplier.

frequency/times-period.description: Number of times per period. E.g. 2 times per day, 1 time per week.

times.description: Number of intakes per period (e.g. 2 for 'twice a day')

period.description: Length of the period (e.g. 1 for 'per day', 8 for 'every 8 hours')

settings/preferred-locales.description: User preferred locales, ordered by priority

delivery/composite-v1.description: Per-baby delivery / birth event. One event per baby; siblings of multifetal pregnancies share event.time (= delivery date) and typically share 'mode'. Stillbirth status is carried upstream on fertility-pregnancy.outcome=stillbirth, NOT here. Field vocabularies aligned to LOINC: 73762-7 (mode), 8339-4 (birth weight), 76689-9 (sex assigned at birth), 9274-2 (5-min APGAR).

mode.description: Final route and method of delivery (LOINC 73762-7 / US Standard Certificate of Live Birth).

sex.description: Sex assigned at birth (LOINC 76689-9). 'indeterminate' covers ambiguous genitalia / disorders of sex development.

nicuLengthOfStayDays.description: Length of NICU admission in days; 0 if not admitted.

neonatalDeathDays.description: Days from delivery to neonatal death (within 28 days post-livebirth). Absent / null = alive at last follow-up.

cervix-position/3d-vectors.description: Cervical position observation as up to four 0–1 vectors: height (low→high), firmness (firm→soft), openness (closed→open) follow the SHOW fertility direction (0 = least fertile, 1 = most fertile). Optional tilt (straight→tilted) is a position-only field, not a fertility signal — RYB-originated; no clinical-coding convention exists.

openness.description: Cervical os openness: 0 = closed, 0.5 = medium, 1 = open

tilt.description: Cervix tilt: 0 = straight, 0.5 = medium, 1 = tilted. Position-only — not a fertility signal. Added in body-vulva-cervix-position v2 (RYB-originated; no clinical-coding convention).

mood/5d-vectors.description: Mood observation as five 0–1 dimensional vectors based on Russell's Circumplex and PAD models. Content is { vectors, source? }. Used with the euclidian-distance converter engine.

valence.description: Pleasure/displeasure: 0 = very unpleasant, 0.5 = neutral, 1 = very pleasant

arousal.description: Energy/activation: 0 = very calm, 0.5 = moderate, 1 = very activated

dominance.description: Sense of control: 0 = powerless, 0.5 = neutral, 1 = in control

source.description: Provenance: which method produced this vector. Absent = direct vector input.

key.description: Source method identifier (e.g. mira, hds, descriptive)

sourceData.description: Raw observation from the source method

message/system-alert.description: Operator → user notification on app-system-out. Content: { level, title, body, ackRequired?, ackId? }. Plan 45.

ackRequired.description: If true, the user's app surfaces an Acknowledge button and the operator expects a matching message/system-ack

ackId.description: UUID generated by the operator at post time. Echoed in the matching message/system-ack to link the response.

message/system-ack.description: User → operator acknowledgement of a message/system-alert, paired by ackId. Plan 45.

ackedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp at which the user clicked Acknowledge

userNote.description: Optional free-text note from the user (max 1000 chars)

questionnaire/request-v1.description: Doctor (or generator) → patient: a questionnaire instantiated for this patient. Carries a 'questions' map keyed by stable question keys. Each question references an existing HDS item (itemRef) with optional entity filter (params), a temporal scope (ever / window / latest with withinDays), and an optional subField for qualifier capture. The patient's matching questionnaire/answer-v1 event carries the answers map keyed by the same question keys and links back via requestEventId. Question keys must match Pryv content-query path grammar [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+ so content.answers.<key>.status queries work. See documentation/QUESTIONNAIRE.md.

title.description: Optional localized title shown to the patient

description.description: Optional localized description / instructions shown above the questionnaire

templateRef.description: Optional reference to a saved hds-lib appTemplates Questionnaire definition that this request was generated from

questions.description: Map of questionKey → question definition. Keys must respect the Pryv content-query path grammar [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+ — no colons, dots, or array indices.

label.description: Localized question text shown to the patient

itemRef.description: Key of an existing HDS item (data-model items registry) that defines storage shape + renderer for the answer's referenced events

params.description: Optional filter narrowing the question to a specific entity (e.g. { drug: { codes: [...] } } for a medication question). Shape is interpreted by the referenced item's renderer.

scope.description: Temporal scope evaluated by the renderer for prefill / matching event lookup

subField.description: Optional qualifier capture rendered alongside the answer when status is 'answered'. Stored as 'qualifier' on the answer entry.

questionnaire/answer-v1.description: Patient → doctor: the response to a questionnaire/request-v1. 'requestEventId' links back to the request event. 'answers' is a map keyed by the same question keys as the request; key absence means 'not-answered' (skipped / unreached). Each answer entry carries a 'status' enum (answered / no / unknown / declined) with conditional fields per status. Referenced event ids in 'answered' entries must ALSO be mirrored into clientData.related.<eventId>: true per the Pryv §7 keyed-object cross-reference convention so cohort queries can use the indexed clientData parameter. See documentation/QUESTIONNAIRE.md.

requestEventId.description: eventId of the questionnaire/request-v1 event being answered

answers.description: Map of questionKey → answer entry. Keys present here MUST correspond to questions in the linked request. Key absence = implicit 'not-answered' (skipped or unreached).

references.description: eventIds of the typed events that satisfy this question. Non-empty. Each id MUST also appear as a key in clientData.related (with value true) on the same event.

qualifier.description: Optional sub-field value (trimester, free text, number, etc.). Shape interpreted by the request's subField declaration.

reason.description: Optional free-text reason for declining to answer (v1). Future additive extension may accept a coded value (e.g. SNOMED 715169006 'Declined to disclose information').

name.fr: Won de la Corée du Nord

description.en: User-scoped settings for HDS client apps. Holds one event per feature (distinguished by eventType, e.g. settings/hds-react-timeline). Single recurring event per type, updated in place.
description.fr: Paramètres propres à l'utilisateur pour les applications client HDS. Contient un événement par fonctionnalité (distingué par eventType, ex. settings/hds-react-timeline). Un seul événement récurrent par type, mis à jour sur place.
